title: Antibody Purification and Labeling
author: Maya Brewer
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.667hhhn
materials: Antibodies should be in a buffer free of any amine-containing compounds (glycine, Tris, or ammonium ions) and stabilizing proteins (bovine serum albumin). These compounds will interfere with the labeling reaction.
Empty collection tube containing column buffer.

steps.title: For all preparations
steps.instruction: For all preparations: this is to remove BSA, azide, or glycine that are often added by the manufacturer. If the antibody has nothing added, skip the purification step.

steps.title: Assemble Amicon Pro Affinity Concentrator by...
steps.instruction: Assemble Amicon Pro Affinity Concentrator by carefully attaching the Amicon Ultra device to the exchange device. Amicon Pro Purification System.

steps.title: Once the concentrator is assembled, remove...
steps.instruction: Once the concentrator is assembled, remove the collection tube cap, lift the exchange device cap, and add 1 mL of 1X PBS to moisten the cellulose membrane of the Amicon Ultra device.

steps.title: This wash will ensure that the...
steps.instruction: This wash will ensure that the antibody does not stick to the membrane upon its addition.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 4000 x g for 3 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 4000 x g for 3 minutes.

steps.title: Add desired amount of antibody to the exchange device
steps.instruction: Add desired amount of antibody to the exchange device.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 10 minutes at 4000...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 10 minutes at 4000 x g in a swinging-bucket rotor.

steps.title: Add 1 mL of PBS to...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of PBS to the device and centrifuge again at 4000 x g for 10 minutes twice, discarding the flow through each time.

steps.title: Collect purified antibody from the device by reverse spin
steps.instruction: Collect purified antibody from the device by reverse spin.

steps.title: Place a collection tube on top of the Amicon Ultra-0
steps.instruction: Place a collection tube on top of the Amicon Ultra-0.5 device. Make sure flow through has been removed from bottom tube.

steps.title: Invert the assembly and centrifuge in...
steps.instruction: Invert the assembly and centrifuge in a fixed-angle rotor at 1000 x g for 2 minutes.

steps.title: Use a nanodrop to measure the...
steps.instruction: Use a nanodrop to measure the purified antibody at an absorbance of 280 nm.

steps.title: Make sure that the sampling arm...
steps.instruction: Make sure that the sampling arm on the nanodrop is up.

steps.title: Using a P10 pipette, add 1...
steps.instruction: Using a P10 pipette, add 1 µL of the sample onto the lower measurement pedestal.

steps.title: Lower the sampling arm and measure...
steps.instruction: Lower the sampling arm and measure the absorbance of the antibody at 280 nm (A280). The concentration should be given in mg/mL.

steps.title: If labeling 100 ug or less...
steps.instruction: If labeling 100 ug or less of antibody, stop here and follow procedure from Antibody Labeling Kit.

steps.title: If you have more than 100...
steps.instruction: If you have more than 100 ug of antibody, continue to the next section.

steps.title: Dissolve amine-reactive compound (NHS ester) in...
steps.instruction: Dissolve amine-reactive compound (NHS ester) in DMSO or DMF at 10 mg/mL (i.e. 100 µL of DMSO is added to 1 mg compound).

steps.title: Mix the solution by vortex
steps.instruction: Mix the solution by vortex.

steps.title: Aliquot solution into small Eppendorf tubes...
steps.instruction: Aliquot solution into small Eppendorf tubes and store at -80C if all dye is not used during reaction.

steps.title: For successful labeling, protein concentration should...
steps.instruction: For successful labeling, protein concentration should definitely not be less than 2mg/ml, ideally 5-20 mg/mL.

steps.title: Add sodium bicarbonate to the antibody...
steps.instruction: Add sodium bicarbonate to the antibody at a concentration of 0.1 M (i.e. 20 µL of sodium bicarbonate per 200 µg antibody) to make a slightly basic pH for the conjugation to occur.

steps.title: Add 1 µL of the chosen...
steps.instruction: Add 1 µL of the chosen fluorophore NHS ester for each 200 µg of antibody.

steps.title: Labeling of antibody occurs by the...
steps.instruction: Labeling of antibody occurs by the Alexa Fluor dye linking to the primary amine (R-NH 2 ) in peptides and proteins.

steps.title: Wrap the tube in foil and...
steps.instruction: Wrap the tube in foil and incubate at room temperature for 60 minutes on a shaker.

steps.title: Prepare the column for the purification...
steps.instruction: Prepare the column for the purification of the labeled antibody during incubation step.

steps.title: Assemble the spin column from the Antibody Conjugate Purification Kit
steps.instruction: Assemble the spin column from the Antibody Conjugate Purification Kit. Conjugate Purification Column 

steps.title: Remove the column lid
steps.instruction: Remove the column lid.

steps.title: Cut-off square tab at the bottom of the column
steps.instruction: Cut-off square tab at the bottom of the column.

steps.title: Add both frits to the column...
steps.instruction: Add both frits to the column and push them to the bottom of the column using a stir rod or P1000 pipette tip.

steps.title: Use the yellow cap only when filling the column
steps.instruction: Use the yellow cap only when filling the column.

steps.title: Stir the purification resin, then add...
steps.instruction: Stir the purification resin, then add 1 mL of the suspension into the column and allow it to settle by gravity.

steps.title: Continue to add more of the...
steps.instruction: Continue to add more of the suspension until the resin bed volume is about 1.5 mL.

steps.title: Place the spin column in a...
steps.instruction: Place the spin column in a collection tube, and place both in a 15 mL conical tube. Centrifuge at 1100 x g for 3 minutes.

steps.title: Add the sample to the center of the column, dropwise
steps.instruction: Add the sample to the center of the column, dropwise. Allow the solution to absorb into the resin bed.

steps.title: Place the spin column into the...
steps.instruction: Place the spin column into the empty collection tube and 15 mL conical tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 1100 x g for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 1100 x g for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Collect labeled antibody from the collection tube
steps.instruction: Collect labeled antibody from the collection tube. Aliquot then store at 4˚C short term, and -20˚C long term.

steps.title: The conjugates can survive freeze thawing...
steps.instruction: The conjugates can survive freeze thawing but you will need to evaluate each new antibody you use to make sure. We always evaluate one freeze/thaw cycle by repeating staining.
